methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Aclarity is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Julie Bliss Mullen that has raised $14M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M) alongside 1 relevant public comparable and a broader universe of 8 private frontier-tech peers in climate infrastructure.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression anchors to the $14M total raised and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A progression, implying a $28M valuation. Peer-median benchmarking against the three identified comparables yields a $150M implied value, with peers ranging from $110M to $200M. The divergence between these methods reflects heterogeneity in capital efficiency and growth trajectory within the sector at this stage.

Triangulation across both approaches produces a valuation range of $24M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Aclarity trades toward the capital-efficient end of the peer spectrum or commands peer-median multiples. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and relative technology differentiation versus Graphyte and Fourth Power. Resolution of these factors through operational milestones will likely narrow the range considerably ahead of Series B.
